{
  "claims": [
    "AB 689 will allow survivors of domestic violence to seek help via text and other computer-based technologies.",
    "Expanding the crisis line service to include texting or live chat gives survivors more ways to enter lifesaving services and support.",
    "The Covid-19 Pandemic has caused a dramatic increase in California’s domestic violence rates.",
    "The National Domestic Violence Hotline saw a 9% increase in calls, texts, and chats during the pandemic.",
    "The current requirement for domestic violence centers is limited to phone-based hotlines.",
    "AB 689 modernizes domestic violence shelter requirements to include other technological platforms.",
    "AB 689 allows domestic violence centers to be better equipped to help more victims.",
    "The state will be better able to track and collect more accurate data about domestic abuse as a result of AB 689.",
    "Chat and text-based hotlines are crucial for survivors of domestic violence who cannot safely call a hotline.",
    "Expanding access points to include chat and text-based options is key to breaking down barriers to survivor safety."
